title: armeria maritima var. ciliata
intro: Armeria maritima var. ciliata, also known as sea pink or thrift, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the plantain family (Plumbaginaceae). It is primarily found in coastal areas of Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, and is prized for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and act as an expectorant. The plant has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as to aid in digestion and relieve menstrual cramps. Sea pink can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
